title: Internet Archive Switzerland
content: The Internet Archive Switzerland is a national branch of the Internet Archive, providing digital preservation and access to Swiss cultural and scientific content. It maintains an online library of manuscripts, books, and media, and offers tools such as a search engine and API for researchers and the public. The site serves as a digital repository, preserving Swiss heritage and facilitating research.
eventKey: internet-archive-switzerland
normalizedTitle: internet archive switzerland

title: Killswitch: Per-function short-circuit mitigation primitive
content: "Killswitch" introduces a per‑function short‑circuit mitigation primitive, discussed in a Hacker News thread. It proposes a method to automatically disable or constrain specific functions in a program to mitigate security vulnerabilities. The discussion examines its potential benefits, implementation considerations, and limitations within software protection practices.
eventKey: killswitch-per-function-short-circuit-mitigation-primitive
normalizedTitle: killswitch per-function short-circuit mitigation primitive

title: Reviving the IBM Selectric Composer Fonts (2023)
content: A 2023 discussion on Hacker News highlights an effort to restore the original font set of IBM’s Selectric Composer typewriter. The thread examines the historical significance of these type and character designs and explores technical steps for reviving the original digital font files. It encourages preserving legacy typewriter aesthetics for modern use, notes the challenges of digitizing paper‑based fonts, and suggests possible resources for accessing the original typefaces.

normalizedTitle: reviving the ibm selectric composer fonts 2023

title: masterking32/MasterDnsVPN
content: MasterDNSVPN is a Go‑based DNS tunneling VPN designed to bypass censorship. It improves on DNSTT and SlipStream by adding low‑overhead ARQ, resolver load balancing, and robust stability in high packet‑loss environments while maintaining speed. The project is popular, with 2,082 stars and 215 forks, and focuses on efficient, reliable VPN performance.

title: All my clients wanted a carousel, now it's an AI chatbot
content: Clients increasingly prefer AI chatbots over traditional carousels, as the technology offers more personalized interaction and dynamic content. The trend reflects a shift toward conversational interfaces that adapt to user queries, providing faster, context‑aware responses and reducing repetitive design work. Consequently, designers and developers must prioritize AI integration to meet evolving client demands and enhance user experience.

title: 基于 AI 的 Chrome 爬虫辅助插件
content: AI‑powered Chrome extension automatically captures Fetch/XHR requests and their responses while browsing. It hands each request to AI, which infers the API’s purpose, deciphers JSON field meanings, and explains business logic. Planned features include multi‑language code generation (Python, Go, Node.js), automatic export of TypeScript interfaces, Go structs, and Pydantic models, bulk endpoint documentation, and customizable code templates. The project is open source on GitHub.

title: 2026 05 09 HackerNews
content: Cloudflare announced a 20 % workforce cut (≈1,100 jobs) to reorganise for an “AI‑agent era”, despite Q1 gains and a weaker Q2 outlook that spiked shares. Canvas experienced a data‑breach attack, temporarily shutting down services after a hacker threatened to leak 275 million records; most users have regained access but full restoration is pending. Poland’s GDP exceeded $1 trillion, making it the 20th largest economy, but faces ageing, low birth rates and regional inequality. New security advisories urge temporary software‑install pauses amid rising Linux kernel and supply‑chain exploits, and highlight the threat of low‑quality AI content diluting online communities. Additional stories cover a Pix‑payment surge, Google Cloud Fraud Defence criticisms, privacy‑leak demos, and a 100‑year‑old David Attenborough celebration.
eventKey: 2026-hackernews
normalizedTitle: 2026 05 09 hackernews

title: DeepSeek 拟最多募资 500 亿元
content: DeepSeek plans the largest equity round in Chinese AI history, aiming to raise up to 50 billion yuan (~$6.9 billion). Since April, reports suggested the company is actively seeking funds. Sources say the National Integrated Circuit Industry Investment Fund, Tencent, and Alibaba are negotiating to lead the first external round, potentially valuing DeepSeek at around $45 billion after investment.

title: 苹果带摄像头 AirPods 项目或暂停 欧盟隐私法规成「绊脚石」
content: Apple’s planned AirPods with built‑in cameras have reportedly reached the DVT testing stage, with a possible launch in September alongside a new iPhone. However, supply‑chain reports indicate that some production lines have been halted and the project may have been temporarily put on hold, possibly due to compliance risks posed by EU privacy regulations. The new headset, based on the AirPods Pro 3 and expected to be called AirPods Ultra, would be Apple’s first true AI‑wearable device, featuring a camera on the stem and an LED indicator that illuminates when data is transmitted to the cloud.

content: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang praised a new partnership with Corning, calling it a key opportunity for rebuilding the U.S. tech supply chain. title: CyberSecQwen-4B: Why Defensive Cyber Needs Small, Specialized, Locally-Runnable Models
content: CyberSecQwen‑4B is a 4‑billion‑parameter, instruction‑tuned model fine‑tuned on CVE‑to‑CWE mappings and defensive Q&A to perform cybersecurity tasks such as CWE classification and structured threat‑intelligence queries. Trained on a single AMD MI300X GPU, it achieves CTI‑Bench scores comparable to an 8‑B model while fitting on a 12 GB consumer GPU, enabling local, cost‑effective deployment in air‑gapped environments. The model is released under Apache 2.0 and can be run via Hugging Face or vLLM on any compatible GPU.

title: Ben Horowitz on the Next Technology Era
content: Ben Horowitz discusses the responsibilities of leading technology on a global scale, emphasizing the growing intersection of venture capital, technology, and national strategy. He stresses the importance of maintaining U.S. technological leadership for economic growth and geopolitical influence, covering AI, advanced manufacturing, and national security innovation. Horowitz also reflects on the evolution of venture capital, the shift to larger, specialized firms, and the need for continued optimism about technology’s potential to improve lives.
source: The a16z Show - content+a16zpodcast@a16z.com (Ben Horowitz, David Ulevitch)

title: EMO: Pretraining mixture of experts for emergent modularity
content: EMO is a mixture‑of‑experts language model that learns modularity end‑to‑end. By constraining all tokens in a document to route through a shared subset of experts, the routers form coherent domain groups (e.g., health, code, math). A small subset—12.5% of experts—retains >97% of full‑model performance, while using all experts gives competitive general‑purpose results. EMO outperforms standard MoEs when selectively using experts, and its components can be identified cheaply with few examples. The model, code, and baseline are released for research.
